title: fagopyrum × heterocarpum
intro: Fagopyrum × heterocarpum, also known as buckwheat, is a pseudocereal plant that is not a true wheat but rather a seed produced by a flowering plant. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health. The health benefits of buckwheat include its potential to help manage diabetes, reduce the risk of heart disease, and support digestive health. Buckwheat is often prepared as a tea, or as a supplement in capsule or powder form, and can also be used as a gluten-free flour substitute in cooking.
